summary.intent: Implement two cost-reduction optimizations for an LLM evaluation pipeline: inventory-based evaluator skipping (#2) and empty-result fast-path instructions (#6), out of a larger 5-optimization plan.
summary.outcome: Both optimizations were implemented and verified: fast-path instructions added to output-format prompts and JSON_OUTPUT_REMINDER, and a new inventory-skip-rules module wired into the runner and engine. All 1351 tests pass, lint and typecheck clean, committed as c653b3c.

repo: EvaluatorResult already supports { skipped: true, skipReason: '...' } pattern — no new type changes needed for skip logic
engine.ts has 3 distinct runner call sites that need to receive technicalInventory: unified mode (~line 1504), independent mode (~line 1907), and no-file mode (~line 1122)
technicalInventory is accessed via contextResult?.context?.technicalInventory in engine.ts
Skip decisions should be computed once before the concurrency loop, not per-evaluator, to avoid redundant work
The evaluation pipeline targets: cost < $5.00 (from $7.72), cache hit ratio > 70%, wall-clock < 400s (from 534s)

code.finding: JSON_OUTPUT_REMINDER is appended at end of prompt; updated to include 'If no issues found, return exactly [].' — adding text here pushes prompt length past the 200-char window tested in prompt-builder.test.ts
Tests assert that JSON_OUTPUT_REMINDER appears within last N chars of prompt; threshold had to be raised from 200 to 250 after the reminder text was extended

New module: computeInventorySkips(inventory) returns Map<string, SkipDecision>. DB dep detection covers ~30 packages across JS/Python/Go/Ruby/Java. Security evaluator only skipped for very minimal repos (no env vars, no config files, no deps at all).
Both runAllEvaluators and runUnifiedEvaluation accept technicalInventory in options; skip map is computed once before the evaluator loop via computeInventorySkips()

workflow: Tests run with 'bun run test', lint with 'bun run lint' — both must pass before committing
User reverted changes mid-session to narrow scope; always confirm scope before starting multi-optimization implementations
Prompt-only changes (fast-path instructions) are lower risk and should be verified first before code changes

friction: User interrupted initial implementation (which had started on all 5 optimizations) and requested a revert to focus only on #2 and #6 — scope was not confirmed before starting
3 test failures after updating JSON_OUTPUT_REMINDER because the reminder position threshold in prompt-builder.test.ts was hardcoded to 200 chars and needed to be bumped to 250
Multiple redundant reads of a cached tool-result file when trying to access specific sections of engine.ts, suggesting the file was too large to navigate efficiently
open_items: Optimization #3 (ROOT_ONLY file filtering for project-structure.md, security.md, completeness.md in config.ts) — deferred by user
Optimization #5 (output format template compaction from ~230 to ~80-100 lines) — deferred by user
Optimization #7 (two-phase triage LLM call to pre-select evaluators) — deferred, most complex, intended to be opt-in via --triage flag
A/B testing for triage (#7) to measure false negative rate vs baseline has not been done
Verification against Supabase repo to confirm actual cost/duration reduction has not been performed
